communities.name: AI Tinkerers Geneva
communities.description: New Geneva chapter of the world's largest AI builder community, launching at SDG Solution Space. Follows the AI Tinkerers Lausanne model (400+ engineers engaged at EPFL over 2 years).
communities.whyTarget: Launching May 13, 2026 in Sonar's home city. The chapter launch announcement explicitly names Sonar as part of Geneva's tech ecosystem. No code quality tool currently sponsors any Swiss AI community — founding sponsorship locks in the category and unlocks visibility across the 220+ city global AI Tinkerers network downstream. Time-sensitive.

communities.name: AI Engineer Summit / Code Summit / World's Fair
communities.description: Premier AI engineering conference series. World's Fair (SF, Jun 2025) drew 6,000+ attendees; Code Summit (NYC, Nov 2025) and AI Engineer Europe (London, Apr 2026) cover AI coding, agents, and code quality at depth.
communities.whyTarget: The definitive conference series for AI engineers. AI Engineer Code Summit (Nov 2025, NYC) directly discussed "code review as the ultimate bottleneck" and "AI as a force multiplier that turns weak standards into architectural chaos" — Sonar's exact messaging. Every major AI coding tool competitor is already there; Sonar cannot afford to be absent.

communities.name: AI Tinkerers Global Network
communities.description: World's largest AI builder community with 220+ city chapters and a strict live-code demo format. Tiered sponsorship packages for meetups, VIP dinners, hackathons, and workshops.
communities.whyTarget: 99,000+ screened members across 220+ cities with a code-first, no-slides format — exactly the builders shipping AI-generated code that Sonar cleans up. A single global relationship (or a small portfolio of key chapters) covers Seattle's Dev Tools Track, Paris, Dublin, Berlin, NYC, London, and more. Zero code quality competitors currently sponsor any chapter except CodeRabbit at Atlanta.

communities.name: AI Tinkerers San Francisco
communities.description: Flagship SF chapter with 150+ screened builders per event at rotating venues (GitHub HQ, AWS Loft, Google Cloud). Strict code-first format.

communities.name: AICamp SF Bay Area
communities.description: 50,000+ local AI developers, 500K+ globally. Monthly tech talks with explicit sponsor packages including speaking slots.

events.dateRaw: Host an EventBe part of NYC's first official AI Week — a community-led festival featuring dozens of events across the city: host your own panel, talk, demo, workshop, conference or meetup. There is no fee to participate, and the submission process is quick and straightforward. Read our FAQ for info. Anyone is welcome to submit an AI-related event for inclusion in the AI Week NY 2026 program. Approved events will be featured on the official AI Week NY 2026 master calendar (on our website and Luma) and promoted citywide. Submission Deadline: April 20, 2026

HomeAboutHost an EventFAQEvent CalendarAI Week New YorkMay 11-17, 2026Spring Edition • 50+ Events • 10,000+ AttendeesThe city’s premier, community-driven festival by Pulse NYC dedicated to AI (artificial intelligence) — bringing together C-suite leaders, founders, engineers, investors & policymakers across NYC. More Info is coming up!Host an EventAttend EventsSpring Edition (May 11-17)About AI Week New York 2026AI Week New York (Spring Edition) — May 11–17, 2026, organized by Pulse NYC, is a week-long, citywide tech festival featuring a dynamic series of community-led events, interactive workshops, panels, talks, and exclusive networking opportunities. It offers attendees a front-row seat to how artificial intelligence is transforming business, society, and everyday life. Taking place across NYC, AI Week New York 2026 will bring together hundreds of tech leaders, innovators, and practitioners from government, startups, academia, and the broader tech ecosystem to explore the latest breakthroughs in AI, learn from one another, and drive responsible innovation forward — together. This intimate event, a mix of TedEx, Foo Camp and Summit, curates sessions to bring senior managers, C-suite executives, and the most talented local AI/ML researchers face-to-face, fostering education and collaboration on innovative solutions in AI, machine learning, data science, predictive analytics, and best practices. Expect to walk away with actionable insights from those working on the frontlines of AI and machine learning in finance, insurance and technology. Rowe Price Moody Hadi Head of Credit Analytics New Product Development at S&P Global Sassoon Kosian Corporate Vice President, Data Science at New York Life Insurance Company Jayeeta Putatunda Senior Data Scientist at Fitch Ratings Chong Dang Lead Machine Learning Engineer at Freddie Mac Elena Ferri Data Science and Advanced Analytics Lead at Federal Reserve Bank of New York About ALD New York AI Loves Data New York is a one-day, invite-only experience for senior managers, C-suite executives, and top local AI/ML researchers. This exclusive event is all about networking and connecting you with the right people you want to meet. It features a curated mix of use-cases, technical talks, workshops, and Q&A sessions, designed to foster direct interaction and collaboration among industry leaders. Apply to attend and join us to gain actionable insights from those at the forefront of machine learning in the enterprise. apply to speak Watch Past Sessions WHY ATTEND THE ALD NYC EXPERIENCE? Connect with Leaders Shaping Enterprise AIMeet and network with senior data scientists, engineering leads, product strategists, and C-suite executives from top financial institutions, tech companies, and AI-first startups. ALD NYC fosters an intentionally curated environment for high-quality, peer-level exchange. Engage in Technical, Purpose-Driven SessionsGo beyond surface-level content. Explore real-world case studies, deep technical walkthroughs, and interactive Q&A sessions focused on deploying Generative AI, LLMOps, and intelligent agents in complex enterprise environments. Learn from Enterprise TrailblazersHear directly from those building and governing AI systems across industries like banking, fintech, healthcare, and cloud infrastructure. Discover how they’re solving real problems—from hallucination and latency to compliance and auditability. Ask the Questions That MatterGet honest, detailed insights from the architects of real-world systems—not sales pitches. Our sessions are built to encourage dialogue, challenge assumptions, and support practical problem-solving. Leave with Actionable InsightsWalk away with proven strategies and technical takeaways—from agentic system design and retrieval architecture to cost-efficient scaling, evaluation frameworks, and cross-functional governance models. Grab Your Pass Top attending companies  Venue S&P Global Market Intelligence, 55 Water St, New York, NY 10041 Purchase a Pass to ALD NYC 2025 GRAB YOUR EARLY BIRD PASS Refund and Ticket Transfer Policy Refunds are available up to 30 days prior to the start of the event.Tickets may be transferred to another attendee up to 48 hours before the event.No exceptions. Expect to walk away with actionable insights from those working on the frontlines of AI and machine learning in finance, insurance and technology. Rowe Price Moody Hadi Head of Credit Analytics New Product Development at S&P Global Sassoon Kosian Corporate Vice President, Data Science at New York Life Insurance Company Jayeeta Putatunda Senior Data Scientist at Fitch Ratings Chong Dang Lead Machine Learning Engineer at Freddie Mac Elena Ferri Data Science and Advanced Analytics Lead at Federal Reserve Bank of New York About ALD New York AI Loves Data New York is a one-day, invite-only experience for senior managers, C-suite executives, and top local AI/ML researchers. This exclusive event is all about networking and connecting you with the right people you want to meet. It features a curated mix of use-cases, technical talks, workshops, and Q&A sessions, designed to foster direct interaction and collaboration among industry leaders. Apply to attend and join us to gain actionable insights from those at the forefront of machine learning in the enterprise. apply to speak Watch Past Sessions WHY ATTEND THE ALD NYC EXPERIENCE? Connect with Leaders Shaping Enterprise AIMeet and network with senior data scientists, engineering leads, product strategists, and C-suite executives from top financial institutions, tech companies, and AI-first startups. ALD NYC fosters an intentionally curated environment for high-quality, peer-level exchange. Engage in Technical, Purpose-Driven SessionsGo beyond surface-level content. Explore real-world case studies, deep technical walkthroughs, and interactive Q&A sessions focused on deploying Generative AI, LLMOps, and intelligent agents in complex enterprise environments. Learn from Enterprise TrailblazersHear directly from those building and governing AI systems across industries like banking, fintech, healthcare, and cloud infrastructure. Discover how they’re solving real problems—from hallucination and latency to compliance and auditability. Ask the Questions That MatterGet honest, detailed insights from the architects of real-world systems—not sales pitches. Our sessions are built to encourage dialogue, challenge assumptions, and support practical problem-solving. Leave with Actionable InsightsWalk away with proven strategies and technical takeaways—from agentic system design and retrieval architecture to cost-efficient scaling, evaluation frameworks, and cross-functional governance models. events.dateRaw: eMerge Americas is a catalyst, an accelerator, and a global tech conference + expo driving innovative advancements in AI, National Security, Health, and Finance. Join Us in Miami, FL | April 22-24, 2026 eMerge AI + deep tech eMerge NATIONAL SECURITY eMerge HEALTH eMerge FINANCE The Premier Global Tech Conference + Expo The Only Cross-Industry Event for AI + Deep Tech, National Security, Health, and Finance Join Us in Miami, FL | April 22-24, 2026 20 k+ attendees 60 + countries 250 + Speakers 300+ Exhibitors WHY ATTEND? Join the World’s Boldest Thinkers eMerge Americas is a strategic convener and catalyst for innovation, bringing together global enterprises, startups, investors, and government leaders to accelerate advancements in AI + Deep Tech, National Security, Health, and Finance. Through year-round programming and our annual global tech conference + expo in Miami, eMerge Americas connects capital, talent, and ideas to drive impactful progress, strengthen industries, and bring transformative and emerging technologies to market. REGISTER NOW VIEW AGENDA 76% Senior Executives 1,000+ Investors 2,500 + Startups $3B Capital Raised 2023 Highlights This is a headline introducing highlights from last year More highlights Event speakers Meet some of our speakers headline can fit on multiple lines of text More speakers WORLD-CLASS THOUGHT LEADERS 2026 Speakers Emil Michael Under Secretary of War for Research and Engineering Department of War Tim Tebow Entrepreneur, College Football Analyst for ESPN + SEC Network, and 6x New York Times Best-Selling Author Dr. Alex Oshmyansky Co-founder + CEO Mark Cuban Cost Plus Drug Company Jamie Siminoff Founder Ring Col. Nicole Malachowski, USAF (Ret.) First Woman Thunderbird Pilot, Combat Veteran, Fighter Squadron Commander, White House Fellow & Adviser Ali Dasdan Chief Technology Officer Dropbox Manish Goyal VP & Senior Partner, Global AI & Analytics Leader IBM Consulting Nicole Baer Chief Marketing Officer Carta Mariana Atencio Award-Winning Journalist + Author GoLike Media Eric Trump Co-Founder + Chief Strategy Officer American Bitcoin MORE SPEAKERS Powering Innovation Together Partner with us to showcase your brand alongside the forefront of global innovation. eMerge Americas offers influential exposure, networking, and collaboration opportunities. Become a driving force in shaping the future of technology. PARTNER WITH US EXPLORE Dive Into a Unique Experience Step into the forefront of innovation across four dynamic sectors reshaping our future: AI + Deep Tech, Health, National Security, and Finance. Each delivers immersive experiences, cutting-edge tech demonstrations, insightful panels, and headline keynotes, while unlocking unmatched opportunities for cross-industry networking. Choose a path below to explore more. eMerge AI + Deep Tech eMerge NATSEC eMerge HEALTH eMerge FINANCE 2025 HIGHLIGHTS Interact with top tech companies: From founders and powerhouse CEOs to government leaders and today’s top tech minds, eMerge Americas is where bold ideas collide and changemakers across tech, business, and culture connect, create, and ignite what’s next. Meet the Movers & Shakers Spot the Next Unicorn Tech by Day. Miami Vibes by Night. Network with global tech leaders, investors, and innovators 76% of attendees are senior-level executives 60+ countries represented Expanding international pavilions with strong LatAm, Europe and Asia presence #1 region in North America for investment Explore 300+ exhibitors showcasing disruptive solutions Interactive demos from leading companies and startups Hands-on experiences with emerging technologies Cross-industry representation from Fortune 500 to early stage Gain insights from 250+ world-class speakers and keynotes Visionaries from tech, policy, venture capital, and beyond Live Pitch Competition featuring the world’s most promising founders Engage with the eMerge Americas Startup Accelerator + Showcase cohort Explore with cutting-edge content across four high-impact verticals (Health, AI, Finance, and National Security) Programming tailored to industry disruption and collaboration Exclusive year-round content tracks and activations Top thought leaders shaping the future of each sector Connect with startups and VCs in the eMerge Startup Accelerator + Showcase 100+ pre-vetted, high-growth startups 6th in the nation for number of deals closed Curated pitch events, mentor sessions, and investor matchmaking Experience Miami’s vibrant innovation and cultural scene Hosted in the heart of Florida’s scaling tech hub Live music concert, opening night block party, and VIP afterhours events The perfect collision of business, fun, and bold ideas Who Attends eMerge Americas? The eMerge experience is powered by people on the front lines of innovation. From C-suite leaders and startup founders to policymakers, our community represents every corner of the tech ecosystem. communities.description: Six additional Swiss communities: Zurich AI Club (1,580 members at AWS Zurich and ETH AI Center); Zurich AI Festival (Sep 29–Oct 4, 2025, 5,000+ attendees across 25+ events, organized by ETH AI Center); Generative AI Genève (318 members, growing); Mindstone Geneva (monthly); YAIL Zurich (monthly + dinners with Bern/Geneva/Lausanne hubs); AI Suisse (Switzerland's largest AI community, first Swiss DeepLearning.AI ambassador).
communities.groupName: Data Science Festival London / Alan Turing Institute / AI Safety London / Data and AI Leaders UK / Futures Forum AI / Mindstone London

communities.name: AI Tinkerers Dublin
communities.description: Co-organized by Esther Trapadoux (Head of Community, Zed Industries). Highest concentration of AI coding tool involvement in Europe.
notableCompanies: Lovable  used by winning hackathon teams with OpenAI

communities.name: Oxford AI Society (OXAI) / Oxbridge AI Challenge

contact.value: Contact via oxai.org; Oxbridge AI Challenge sponsorship via site

communities.description: University-affiliated society with AI Gala at Natural History Museum. Oxbridge AI Challenge offers £60K prizes + £150K Azure credits.

communities.name: Manchester Tech Festival

contact.value: Sponsorship packages for 2026 published on site

communities.description: Multi-day festival (Sep 23–Oct 3, 2025). AI Conference Oct 1.

communities.name: Mindstone
communities.description: Monthly three-talk AI meetup network across London, Bristol, Leeds, Cambridge, Newcastle, Amsterdam, Berlin, Zurich, Geneva, Madrid, Lisbon.
notableCompanies: Cursor  referenced in London speaker talks

communities.description: Cambridge AI has 1,475 members at Raspberry Pi Foundation HQ; Reading Group (323 members) meets at The Castle Inn; ai@cam is the University initiative that hosted NeurIPS meetup@Cambridge Dec 2025.

Vercel 🔧 provides swag/credits/food stipends via Meetup SDK; HF hosts mega meetups (5K+ in SF); Pinecone runs meetups at a16z offices across SF/NYC/Seattle + $100K+ hackathons; Groq sponsors hackathons (UCSD, AI Tinkerers, India); LlamaIndex co-hosts with AICamp and Snowflake; Weaviate runs Berlin hackathons, NYC "Whiskey & Wisdom," CALIBRATE conference with Comet at AWS GenAI Loft.
communities.groupName: Vercel Meetup SDK / Hugging Face Global / Pinecone / Groq / LlamaIndex / Weaviate

communities.name: Women in AI (WAI)
communities.description: Six diversity-focused AI communities: WAI (20,000+ members, 130+ countries, 40+ chapters, June 2025 NA Summit 500+); WiMLDS (~100 chapters, 50,000+ members, 501(c)(3), NYC chapter 4,777 members); Black in AI (5,900+ members, 117 countries, NeurIPS workshop, MacArthur grant); LatinX in AI (NeurIPS/ICRA/ACL workshops); Queer in AI (1,000+ Slack, ACL first archival papers 2025); AI4ALL (Dr. Fei-Fei Li co-founded, Stanford/UCSF summer programs).

} } Our Programs. LEARN MORE #block-459a67713ae5b05a35d0 {--sqs-block-content-flex: 0; } Research Workshops at Top AI Conferences LatinX in AI hosts flagship research workshops at premier AI conferences—including NeurIPS, ICML, CVPR, and NAACL—to amplify the voices and contributions of LatinX researchers on the global stage. These workshops provide a welcoming platform for scholars at all stages to present original research, gain visibility, and engage in technical dialogue with the broader AI community.Each workshop features:Peer-reviewed research papers spanning machine learning, computer vision, NLP, ethics, and moreInvited talks from leading LatinX scientists and industry pioneersMentoring sessions, panels, and networking designed to build community and advance careersThrough these workshops, LatinX in AI has built a recognized space for collaboration, visibility, and scholarly excellence—helping hundreds of researchers develop confidence, connect with peers, and access global opportunities in AI. Mentorship That Transforms Careers LatinX in AI’s Mentorship Program connects emerging LatinX talent with experienced researchers, engineers, founders, and industry professionals across the globe. Our goal is to create pathways for growth, confidence, and opportunity in the field of AI.Through structured 1:1 mentorship, group sessions, and peer circles, we support mentees in areas such as:Graduate school applications and academic publishingCareer transitions and technical interviewsResearch feedback and professional developmentWith hundreds of matches made across borders and disciplines, our mentorship network is a cornerstone of our mission—building bridges between generations of LatinX AI leaders and cultivating a more inclusive future for artificial intelligence. Journal of LatinX in AI Research (JLXAIR) The LatinX in AI Research Journal is an open-access publication dedicated to showcasing original work from LatinX researchers across the artificial intelligence landscape. Our journal provides a platform for emerging voices, interdisciplinary ideas, and socially impactful research that often goes underrepresented in mainstream venues.Through regular issues and conference-aligned special editions, we feature:Peer-reviewed papers spanning machine learning, ethics, computer vision, NLP, and moreStudent and early-career research highlightsCollaborative works between academia, industry, and civil societyBy centering LatinX perspectives in AI, our journal not only elevates scholarship but also builds visibility, confidence, and academic credibility for contributors—especially those from developing countries and underfunded institutions. Supercomputer Access Network Through the LXAI Supercomputer Access Network, we provide LatinX researchers, students, and educators with free or low-cost access to cutting-edge AI computing infrastructure—including GPU clusters and supercomputing resources—via partnerships with academic institutions, industry donors, and national labs.This initiative addresses one of the most critical barriers in AI research: lack of access to high-performance computing. Our network enables under-resourced scholars, particularly from Latin America and other developing regions, to:Train large-scale AI modelsRun complex experimentsCollaborate on globally competitive research projectsBy democratizing access to compute, we’re leveling the playing field and fostering a new generation of LatinX innovators in artificial intelligence. LXAI Podcast: Voices of the LatinX AI Community The LatinX in AI Podcast is a storytelling platform that amplifies the voices of LatinX leaders, researchers, entrepreneurs, and changemakers in the field of artificial intelligence. Through in-depth interviews and topical discussions, we explore the intersections of technology, identity, equity, and innovation—bringing forward stories that are too often left out of the AI narrative.Each episode highlights:Breakthrough research and startup journeysInsights into ethical AI, policy, and social impactThe lived experiences of LatinX professionals navigating the tech worldOur podcast builds connection across borders and disciplines while inspiring the next generation of LatinX talent. Whether you’re in academia, industry, or just starting out, the LXAI Podcast offers valuable perspectives from across our global community. LATAM Undergraduate Research Program In collaboration with Google Explore CSR and LatinX in AI, the LATAM Undergraduate Research Program empowers underrepresented undergraduate (and master’s-level) students in computer science and related fields. Through a semester-long mentorship and project-based experience, students develop confidence, critical research skills, and a stronger sense of belonging in the AI community.What participants can expect:Hands-on research projects under the guidance of experienced mentorsOnline workshops, training, and peer activities designed to improve research methods, technical skills, and academic readinessSupport aimed at motivating students to pursue graduate studies in computing and AIThis program not only equips students with practical experience but also strengthens their identity as researchers and accelerates their academic and professional journey in AI. .fe-68c23ca7b0cea560b27e5687 { --grid-gutter: calc(var(--sqs-mobile-site-gutter, 6vw) - 11.0px); } } 1,100+ Grants Awarded Every LatinX in AI research workshop is made possible by generous partnerships with individuals and companies committed to equity in AI. Together, we raise funds for the LXAI Travel and Registration Grant Program, which helps remove financial barriers for underrepresented scholars.Our grants are primarily awarded to students from Latin American countries and Hispanic Serving Institutions in the United States—many of whom would not be able to attend without this support. These grants help cover critical costs like travel, lodging, and conference registration, ensuring that brilliant minds from developing regions can present their work, network, and grow their careers.To date, LatinX in AI has awarded over 1,100 conference and travel grants, directly enabling the participation of LatinX researchers and students in the global AI conversation. Our Impact. 10,000+ Members Served LatinX in AI is proud to serve a vibrant, growing network of over 10,000 LatinX researchers, engineers, entrepreneurs, and students across the globe. Through our targeted programs, we are breaking down systemic barriers and expanding opportunities in the field of artificial intelligence.Our community members benefit from:- Research Workshops at top-tier AI conferences- 1:1 and group mentorship across academia and industry- Collaborative publications via our open-access research journal- Access to AI compute resources for under-resourced researchers and studentsTogether, these programs form a powerful ecosystem for professional growth, academic advancement, and cross-border collaboration—amplifying LatinX voices and talent in AI worldwide. 30+ Countries Represented LatinX in AI is a truly global community, representing all Latin-identified countries across North, Central, and South America, the Caribbean, and beyond. Our members span academic institutions, tech companies, and research labs in over 30 countries, including Brazil, Mexico, Colombia, Argentina, Peru, Chile, Venezuela, the Dominican Republic, and many others.We also proudly serve LatinX individuals across the diaspora—including those based in the United States, Canada, Spain, and the UK—who identify with and support our mission to create a more inclusive AI ecosystem.By uniting voices from across borders, languages, and cultures, LatinX in AI fosters cross-regional collaboration, shared knowledge, and a collective effort to shape the future of artificial intelligence in a way that reflects the diversity of our world. .fe-68c2358ee1f8ff7b36c5c3f0 { --grid-gutter: calc(var(--sqs-mobile-site-gutter, 6vw) - 11.0px); communities.description: Six diversity-focused AI communities: WAI (20,000+ members, 130+ countries, 40+ chapters, June 2025 NA Summit 500+); WiMLDS (~100 chapters, 50,000+ members, 501(c)(3), NYC chapter 4,777 members); Black in AI (5,900+ members, 117 countries, NeurIPS workshop, MacArthur grant); LatinX in AI (NeurIPS/ICRA/ACL workshops); Queer in AI (1,000+ Slack, ACL first archival papers 2025); AI4ALL (Dr. Fei-Fei Li co-founded, Stanford/UCSF summer programs).

} } Our Programs. LEARN MORE #block-459a67713ae5b05a35d0 {--sqs-block-content-flex: 0; } Research Workshops at Top AI Conferences LatinX in AI hosts flagship research workshops at premier AI conferences—including NeurIPS, ICML, CVPR, and NAACL—to amplify the voices and contributions of LatinX researchers on the global stage. These workshops provide a welcoming platform for scholars at all stages to present original research, gain visibility, and engage in technical dialogue with the broader AI community.Each workshop features:Peer-reviewed research papers spanning machine learning, computer vision, NLP, ethics, and moreInvited talks from leading LatinX scientists and industry pioneersMentoring sessions, panels, and networking designed to build community and advance careersThrough these workshops, LatinX in AI has built a recognized space for collaboration, visibility, and scholarly excellence—helping hundreds of researchers develop confidence, connect with peers, and access global opportunities in AI. Mentorship That Transforms Careers LatinX in AI’s Mentorship Program connects emerging LatinX talent with experienced researchers, engineers, founders, and industry professionals across the globe. Our goal is to create pathways for growth, confidence, and opportunity in the field of AI.Through structured 1:1 mentorship, group sessions, and peer circles, we support mentees in areas such as:Graduate school applications and academic publishingCareer transitions and technical interviewsResearch feedback and professional developmentWith hundreds of matches made across borders and disciplines, our mentorship network is a cornerstone of our mission—building bridges between generations of LatinX AI leaders and cultivating a more inclusive future for artificial intelligence. Journal of LatinX in AI Research (JLXAIR) The LatinX in AI Research Journal is an open-access publication dedicated to showcasing original work from LatinX researchers across the artificial intelligence landscape. Our journal provides a platform for emerging voices, interdisciplinary ideas, and socially impactful research that often goes underrepresented in mainstream venues.Through regular issues and conference-aligned special editions, we feature:Peer-reviewed papers spanning machine learning, ethics, computer vision, NLP, and moreStudent and early-career research highlightsCollaborative works between academia, industry, and civil societyBy centering LatinX perspectives in AI, our journal not only elevates scholarship but also builds visibility, confidence, and academic credibility for contributors—especially those from developing countries and underfunded institutions. Supercomputer Access Network Through the LXAI Supercomputer Access Network, we provide LatinX researchers, students, and educators with free or low-cost access to cutting-edge AI computing infrastructure—including GPU clusters and supercomputing resources—via partnerships with academic institutions, industry donors, and national labs.This initiative addresses one of the most critical barriers in AI research: lack of access to high-performance computing. Our network enables under-resourced scholars, particularly from Latin America and other developing regions, to:Train large-scale AI modelsRun complex experimentsCollaborate on globally competitive research projectsBy democratizing access to compute, we’re leveling the playing field and fostering a new generation of LatinX innovators in artificial intelligence. LXAI Podcast: Voices of the LatinX AI Community The LatinX in AI Podcast is a storytelling platform that amplifies the voices of LatinX leaders, researchers, entrepreneurs, and changemakers in the field of artificial intelligence. Through in-depth interviews and topical discussions, we explore the intersections of technology, identity, equity, and innovation—bringing forward stories that are too often left out of the AI narrative.Each episode highlights:Breakthrough research and startup journeysInsights into ethical AI, policy, and social impactThe lived experiences of LatinX professionals navigating the tech worldOur podcast builds connection across borders and disciplines while inspiring the next generation of LatinX talent. Whether you’re in academia, industry, or just starting out, the LXAI Podcast offers valuable perspectives from across our global community. LATAM Undergraduate Research Program In collaboration with Google Explore CSR and LatinX in AI, the LATAM Undergraduate Research Program empowers underrepresented undergraduate (and master’s-level) students in computer science and related fields. Through a semester-long mentorship and project-based experience, students develop confidence, critical research skills, and a stronger sense of belonging in the AI community.What participants can expect:Hands-on research projects under the guidance of experienced mentorsOnline workshops, training, and peer activities designed to improve research methods, technical skills, and academic readinessSupport aimed at motivating students to pursue graduate studies in computing and AIThis program not only equips students with practical experience but also strengthens their identity as researchers and accelerates their academic and professional journey in AI. .fe-68c23ca7b0cea560b27e5687 { --grid-gutter: calc(var(--sqs-mobile-site-gutter, 6vw) - 11.0px); } } 1,100+ Grants Awarded Every LatinX in AI research workshop is made possible by generous partnerships with individuals and companies committed to equity in AI. Together, we raise funds for the LXAI Travel and Registration Grant Program, which helps remove financial barriers for underrepresented scholars.Our grants are primarily awarded to students from Latin American countries and Hispanic Serving Institutions in the United States—many of whom would not be able to attend without this support. These grants help cover critical costs like travel, lodging, and conference registration, ensuring that brilliant minds from developing regions can present their work, network, and grow their careers.To date, LatinX in AI has awarded over 1,100 conference and travel grants, directly enabling the participation of LatinX researchers and students in the global AI conversation. Our Impact. 10,000+ Members Served LatinX in AI is proud to serve a vibrant, growing network of over 10,000 LatinX researchers, engineers, entrepreneurs, and students across the globe. Through our targeted programs, we are breaking down systemic barriers and expanding opportunities in the field of artificial intelligence.Our community members benefit from:- Research Workshops at top-tier AI conferences- 1:1 and group mentorship across academia and industry- Collaborative publications via our open-access research journal- Access to AI compute resources for under-resourced researchers and studentsTogether, these programs form a powerful ecosystem for professional growth, academic advancement, and cross-border collaboration—amplifying LatinX voices and talent in AI worldwide. 30+ Countries Represented LatinX in AI is a truly global community, representing all Latin-identified countries across North, Central, and South America, the Caribbean, and beyond. Our members span academic institutions, tech companies, and research labs in over 30 countries, including Brazil, Mexico, Colombia, Argentina, Peru, Chile, Venezuela, the Dominican Republic, and many others.We also proudly serve LatinX individuals across the diaspora—including those based in the United States, Canada, Spain, and the UK—who identify with and support our mission to create a more inclusive AI ecosystem.By uniting voices from across borders, languages, and cultures, LatinX in AI fosters cross-regional collaboration, shared knowledge, and a collective effort to shape the future of artificial intelligence in a way that reflects the diversity of our world. .fe-68c2358ee1f8ff7b36c5c3f0 { --grid-gutter: calc(var(--sqs-mobile-site-gutter, 6vw) - 11.0px); communities.description: Six diversity-focused AI communities: WAI (20,000+ members, 130+ countries, 40+ chapters, June 2025 NA Summit 500+); WiMLDS (~100 chapters, 50,000+ members, 501(c)(3), NYC chapter 4,777 members); Black in AI (5,900+ members, 117 countries, NeurIPS workshop, MacArthur grant); LatinX in AI (NeurIPS/ICRA/ACL workshops); Queer in AI (1,000+ Slack, ACL first archival papers 2025); AI4ALL (Dr. Fei-Fei Li co-founded, Stanford/UCSF summer programs).

} } Our Programs. LEARN MORE #block-459a67713ae5b05a35d0 {--sqs-block-content-flex: 0; } Research Workshops at Top AI Conferences LatinX in AI hosts flagship research workshops at premier AI conferences—including NeurIPS, ICML, CVPR, and NAACL—to amplify the voices and contributions of LatinX researchers on the global stage. These workshops provide a welcoming platform for scholars at all stages to present original research, gain visibility, and engage in technical dialogue with the broader AI community.Each workshop features:Peer-reviewed research papers spanning machine learning, computer vision, NLP, ethics, and moreInvited talks from leading LatinX scientists and industry pioneersMentoring sessions, panels, and networking designed to build community and advance careersThrough these workshops, LatinX in AI has built a recognized space for collaboration, visibility, and scholarly excellence—helping hundreds of researchers develop confidence, connect with peers, and access global opportunities in AI. Mentorship That Transforms Careers LatinX in AI’s Mentorship Program connects emerging LatinX talent with experienced researchers, engineers, founders, and industry professionals across the globe. Our goal is to create pathways for growth, confidence, and opportunity in the field of AI.Through structured 1:1 mentorship, group sessions, and peer circles, we support mentees in areas such as:Graduate school applications and academic publishingCareer transitions and technical interviewsResearch feedback and professional developmentWith hundreds of matches made across borders and disciplines, our mentorship network is a cornerstone of our mission—building bridges between generations of LatinX AI leaders and cultivating a more inclusive future for artificial intelligence. Journal of LatinX in AI Research (JLXAIR) The LatinX in AI Research Journal is an open-access publication dedicated to showcasing original work from LatinX researchers across the artificial intelligence landscape. Our journal provides a platform for emerging voices, interdisciplinary ideas, and socially impactful research that often goes underrepresented in mainstream venues.Through regular issues and conference-aligned special editions, we feature:Peer-reviewed papers spanning machine learning, ethics, computer vision, NLP, and moreStudent and early-career research highlightsCollaborative works between academia, industry, and civil societyBy centering LatinX perspectives in AI, our journal not only elevates scholarship but also builds visibility, confidence, and academic credibility for contributors—especially those from developing countries and underfunded institutions. Supercomputer Access Network Through the LXAI Supercomputer Access Network, we provide LatinX researchers, students, and educators with free or low-cost access to cutting-edge AI computing infrastructure—including GPU clusters and supercomputing resources—via partnerships with academic institutions, industry donors, and national labs.This initiative addresses one of the most critical barriers in AI research: lack of access to high-performance computing. Our network enables under-resourced scholars, particularly from Latin America and other developing regions, to:Train large-scale AI modelsRun complex experimentsCollaborate on globally competitive research projectsBy democratizing access to compute, we’re leveling the playing field and fostering a new generation of LatinX innovators in artificial intelligence. LXAI Podcast: Voices of the LatinX AI Community The LatinX in AI Podcast is a storytelling platform that amplifies the voices of LatinX leaders, researchers, entrepreneurs, and changemakers in the field of artificial intelligence. Through in-depth interviews and topical discussions, we explore the intersections of technology, identity, equity, and innovation—bringing forward stories that are too often left out of the AI narrative.Each episode highlights:Breakthrough research and startup journeysInsights into ethical AI, policy, and social impactThe lived experiences of LatinX professionals navigating the tech worldOur podcast builds connection across borders and disciplines while inspiring the next generation of LatinX talent. Whether you’re in academia, industry, or just starting out, the LXAI Podcast offers valuable perspectives from across our global community. LATAM Undergraduate Research Program In collaboration with Google Explore CSR and LatinX in AI, the LATAM Undergraduate Research Program empowers underrepresented undergraduate (and master’s-level) students in computer science and related fields. Through a semester-long mentorship and project-based experience, students develop confidence, critical research skills, and a stronger sense of belonging in the AI community.What participants can expect:Hands-on research projects under the guidance of experienced mentorsOnline workshops, training, and peer activities designed to improve research methods, technical skills, and academic readinessSupport aimed at motivating students to pursue graduate studies in computing and AIThis program not only equips students with practical experience but also strengthens their identity as researchers and accelerates their academic and professional journey in AI. .fe-68c23ca7b0cea560b27e5687 { --grid-gutter: calc(var(--sqs-mobile-site-gutter, 6vw) - 11.0px); } } 1,100+ Grants Awarded Every LatinX in AI research workshop is made possible by generous partnerships with individuals and companies committed to equity in AI. Together, we raise funds for the LXAI Travel and Registration Grant Program, which helps remove financial barriers for underrepresented scholars.Our grants are primarily awarded to students from Latin American countries and Hispanic Serving Institutions in the United States—many of whom would not be able to attend without this support. These grants help cover critical costs like travel, lodging, and conference registration, ensuring that brilliant minds from developing regions can present their work, network, and grow their careers.To date, LatinX in AI has awarded over 1,100 conference and travel grants, directly enabling the participation of LatinX researchers and students in the global AI conversation. Our Impact. 10,000+ Members Served LatinX in AI is proud to serve a vibrant, growing network of over 10,000 LatinX researchers, engineers, entrepreneurs, and students across the globe. Through our targeted programs, we are breaking down systemic barriers and expanding opportunities in the field of artificial intelligence.Our community members benefit from:- Research Workshops at top-tier AI conferences- 1:1 and group mentorship across academia and industry- Collaborative publications via our open-access research journal- Access to AI compute resources for under-resourced researchers and studentsTogether, these programs form a powerful ecosystem for professional growth, academic advancement, and cross-border collaboration—amplifying LatinX voices and talent in AI worldwide. 30+ Countries Represented LatinX in AI is a truly global community, representing all Latin-identified countries across North, Central, and South America, the Caribbean, and beyond. Our members span academic institutions, tech companies, and research labs in over 30 countries, including Brazil, Mexico, Colombia, Argentina, Peru, Chile, Venezuela, the Dominican Republic, and many others.We also proudly serve LatinX individuals across the diaspora—including those based in the United States, Canada, Spain, and the UK—who identify with and support our mission to create a more inclusive AI ecosystem.By uniting voices from across borders, languages, and cultures, LatinX in AI fosters cross-regional collaboration, shared knowledge, and a collective effort to shape the future of artificial intelligence in a way that reflects the diversity of our world. .fe-68c2358ee1f8ff7b36c5c3f0 { --grid-gutter: calc(var(--sqs-mobile-site-gutter, 6vw) - 11.0px); communities.description: Six diversity-focused AI communities: WAI (20,000+ members, 130+ countries, 40+ chapters, June 2025 NA Summit 500+); WiMLDS (~100 chapters, 50,000+ members, 501(c)(3), NYC chapter 4,777 members); Black in AI (5,900+ members, 117 countries, NeurIPS workshop, MacArthur grant); LatinX in AI (NeurIPS/ICRA/ACL workshops); Queer in AI (1,000+ Slack, ACL first archival papers 2025); AI4ALL (Dr. Fei-Fei Li co-founded, Stanford/UCSF summer programs).

} } Our Programs. LEARN MORE #block-459a67713ae5b05a35d0 {--sqs-block-content-flex: 0; } Research Workshops at Top AI Conferences LatinX in AI hosts flagship research workshops at premier AI conferences—including NeurIPS, ICML, CVPR, and NAACL—to amplify the voices and contributions of LatinX researchers on the global stage. These workshops provide a welcoming platform for scholars at all stages to present original research, gain visibility, and engage in technical dialogue with the broader AI community.Each workshop features:Peer-reviewed research papers spanning machine learning, computer vision, NLP, ethics, and moreInvited talks from leading LatinX scientists and industry pioneersMentoring sessions, panels, and networking designed to build community and advance careersThrough these workshops, LatinX in AI has built a recognized space for collaboration, visibility, and scholarly excellence—helping hundreds of researchers develop confidence, connect with peers, and access global opportunities in AI. Mentorship That Transforms Careers LatinX in AI’s Mentorship Program connects emerging LatinX talent with experienced researchers, engineers, founders, and industry professionals across the globe. Our goal is to create pathways for growth, confidence, and opportunity in the field of AI.Through structured 1:1 mentorship, group sessions, and peer circles, we support mentees in areas such as:Graduate school applications and academic publishingCareer transitions and technical interviewsResearch feedback and professional developmentWith hundreds of matches made across borders and disciplines, our mentorship network is a cornerstone of our mission—building bridges between generations of LatinX AI leaders and cultivating a more inclusive future for artificial intelligence. Journal of LatinX in AI Research (JLXAIR) The LatinX in AI Research Journal is an open-access publication dedicated to showcasing original work from LatinX researchers across the artificial intelligence landscape. Our journal provides a platform for emerging voices, interdisciplinary ideas, and socially impactful research that often goes underrepresented in mainstream venues.Through regular issues and conference-aligned special editions, we feature:Peer-reviewed papers spanning machine learning, ethics, computer vision, NLP, and moreStudent and early-career research highlightsCollaborative works between academia, industry, and civil societyBy centering LatinX perspectives in AI, our journal not only elevates scholarship but also builds visibility, confidence, and academic credibility for contributors—especially those from developing countries and underfunded institutions. Supercomputer Access Network Through the LXAI Supercomputer Access Network, we provide LatinX researchers, students, and educators with free or low-cost access to cutting-edge AI computing infrastructure—including GPU clusters and supercomputing resources—via partnerships with academic institutions, industry donors, and national labs.This initiative addresses one of the most critical barriers in AI research: lack of access to high-performance computing. Our network enables under-resourced scholars, particularly from Latin America and other developing regions, to:Train large-scale AI modelsRun complex experimentsCollaborate on globally competitive research projectsBy democratizing access to compute, we’re leveling the playing field and fostering a new generation of LatinX innovators in artificial intelligence. LXAI Podcast: Voices of the LatinX AI Community The LatinX in AI Podcast is a storytelling platform that amplifies the voices of LatinX leaders, researchers, entrepreneurs, and changemakers in the field of artificial intelligence. Through in-depth interviews and topical discussions, we explore the intersections of technology, identity, equity, and innovation—bringing forward stories that are too often left out of the AI narrative.Each episode highlights:Breakthrough research and startup journeysInsights into ethical AI, policy, and social impactThe lived experiences of LatinX professionals navigating the tech worldOur podcast builds connection across borders and disciplines while inspiring the next generation of LatinX talent. Whether you’re in academia, industry, or just starting out, the LXAI Podcast offers valuable perspectives from across our global community. LATAM Undergraduate Research Program In collaboration with Google Explore CSR and LatinX in AI, the LATAM Undergraduate Research Program empowers underrepresented undergraduate (and master’s-level) students in computer science and related fields. Through a semester-long mentorship and project-based experience, students develop confidence, critical research skills, and a stronger sense of belonging in the AI community.What participants can expect:Hands-on research projects under the guidance of experienced mentorsOnline workshops, training, and peer activities designed to improve research methods, technical skills, and academic readinessSupport aimed at motivating students to pursue graduate studies in computing and AIThis program not only equips students with practical experience but also strengthens their identity as researchers and accelerates their academic and professional journey in AI. .fe-68c23ca7b0cea560b27e5687 { --grid-gutter: calc(var(--sqs-mobile-site-gutter, 6vw) - 11.0px); } } 1,100+ Grants Awarded Every LatinX in AI research workshop is made possible by generous partnerships with individuals and companies committed to equity in AI. Together, we raise funds for the LXAI Travel and Registration Grant Program, which helps remove financial barriers for underrepresented scholars.Our grants are primarily awarded to students from Latin American countries and Hispanic Serving Institutions in the United States—many of whom would not be able to attend without this support. These grants help cover critical costs like travel, lodging, and conference registration, ensuring that brilliant minds from developing regions can present their work, network, and grow their careers.To date, LatinX in AI has awarded over 1,100 conference and travel grants, directly enabling the participation of LatinX researchers and students in the global AI conversation. Our Impact. 10,000+ Members Served LatinX in AI is proud to serve a vibrant, growing network of over 10,000 LatinX researchers, engineers, entrepreneurs, and students across the globe. Through our targeted programs, we are breaking down systemic barriers and expanding opportunities in the field of artificial intelligence.Our community members benefit from:- Research Workshops at top-tier AI conferences- 1:1 and group mentorship across academia and industry- Collaborative publications via our open-access research journal- Access to AI compute resources for under-resourced researchers and studentsTogether, these programs form a powerful ecosystem for professional growth, academic advancement, and cross-border collaboration—amplifying LatinX voices and talent in AI worldwide. 30+ Countries Represented LatinX in AI is a truly global community, representing all Latin-identified countries across North, Central, and South America, the Caribbean, and beyond. Our members span academic institutions, tech companies, and research labs in over 30 countries, including Brazil, Mexico, Colombia, Argentina, Peru, Chile, Venezuela, the Dominican Republic, and many others.We also proudly serve LatinX individuals across the diaspora—including those based in the United States, Canada, Spain, and the UK—who identify with and support our mission to create a more inclusive AI ecosystem.By uniting voices from across borders, languages, and cultures, LatinX in AI fosters cross-regional collaboration, shared knowledge, and a collective effort to shape the future of artificial intelligence in a way that reflects the diversity of our world. .fe-68c2358ee1f8ff7b36c5c3f0 { --grid-gutter: calc(var(--sqs-mobile-site-gutter, 6vw) - 11.0px); communities.description: Six diversity-focused AI communities: WAI (20,000+ members, 130+ countries, 40+ chapters, June 2025 NA Summit 500+); WiMLDS (~100 chapters, 50,000+ members, 501(c)(3), NYC chapter 4,777 members); Black in AI (5,900+ members, 117 countries, NeurIPS workshop, MacArthur grant); LatinX in AI (NeurIPS/ICRA/ACL workshops); Queer in AI (1,000+ Slack, ACL first archival papers 2025); AI4ALL (Dr. Fei-Fei Li co-founded, Stanford/UCSF summer programs).

} } Our Programs. LEARN MORE #block-459a67713ae5b05a35d0 {--sqs-block-content-flex: 0; } Research Workshops at Top AI Conferences LatinX in AI hosts flagship research workshops at premier AI conferences—including NeurIPS, ICML, CVPR, and NAACL—to amplify the voices and contributions of LatinX researchers on the global stage. These workshops provide a welcoming platform for scholars at all stages to present original research, gain visibility, and engage in technical dialogue with the broader AI community.Each workshop features:Peer-reviewed research papers spanning machine learning, computer vision, NLP, ethics, and moreInvited talks from leading LatinX scientists and industry pioneersMentoring sessions, panels, and networking designed to build community and advance careersThrough these workshops, LatinX in AI has built a recognized space for collaboration, visibility, and scholarly excellence—helping hundreds of researchers develop confidence, connect with peers, and access global opportunities in AI. Mentorship That Transforms Careers LatinX in AI’s Mentorship Program connects emerging LatinX talent with experienced researchers, engineers, founders, and industry professionals across the globe. Our goal is to create pathways for growth, confidence, and opportunity in the field of AI.Through structured 1:1 mentorship, group sessions, and peer circles, we support mentees in areas such as:Graduate school applications and academic publishingCareer transitions and technical interviewsResearch feedback and professional developmentWith hundreds of matches made across borders and disciplines, our mentorship network is a cornerstone of our mission—building bridges between generations of LatinX AI leaders and cultivating a more inclusive future for artificial intelligence. Journal of LatinX in AI Research (JLXAIR) The LatinX in AI Research Journal is an open-access publication dedicated to showcasing original work from LatinX researchers across the artificial intelligence landscape. Our journal provides a platform for emerging voices, interdisciplinary ideas, and socially impactful research that often goes underrepresented in mainstream venues.Through regular issues and conference-aligned special editions, we feature:Peer-reviewed papers spanning machine learning, ethics, computer vision, NLP, and moreStudent and early-career research highlightsCollaborative works between academia, industry, and civil societyBy centering LatinX perspectives in AI, our journal not only elevates scholarship but also builds visibility, confidence, and academic credibility for contributors—especially those from developing countries and underfunded institutions. Supercomputer Access Network Through the LXAI Supercomputer Access Network, we provide LatinX researchers, students, and educators with free or low-cost access to cutting-edge AI computing infrastructure—including GPU clusters and supercomputing resources—via partnerships with academic institutions, industry donors, and national labs.This initiative addresses one of the most critical barriers in AI research: lack of access to high-performance computing. Our network enables under-resourced scholars, particularly from Latin America and other developing regions, to:Train large-scale AI modelsRun complex experimentsCollaborate on globally competitive research projectsBy democratizing access to compute, we’re leveling the playing field and fostering a new generation of LatinX innovators in artificial intelligence. LXAI Podcast: Voices of the LatinX AI Community The LatinX in AI Podcast is a storytelling platform that amplifies the voices of LatinX leaders, researchers, entrepreneurs, and changemakers in the field of artificial intelligence. Through in-depth interviews and topical discussions, we explore the intersections of technology, identity, equity, and innovation—bringing forward stories that are too often left out of the AI narrative.Each episode highlights:Breakthrough research and startup journeysInsights into ethical AI, policy, and social impactThe lived experiences of LatinX professionals navigating the tech worldOur podcast builds connection across borders and disciplines while inspiring the next generation of LatinX talent. Whether you’re in academia, industry, or just starting out, the LXAI Podcast offers valuable perspectives from across our global community. LATAM Undergraduate Research Program In collaboration with Google Explore CSR and LatinX in AI, the LATAM Undergraduate Research Program empowers underrepresented undergraduate (and master’s-level) students in computer science and related fields. Through a semester-long mentorship and project-based experience, students develop confidence, critical research skills, and a stronger sense of belonging in the AI community.What participants can expect:Hands-on research projects under the guidance of experienced mentorsOnline workshops, training, and peer activities designed to improve research methods, technical skills, and academic readinessSupport aimed at motivating students to pursue graduate studies in computing and AIThis program not only equips students with practical experience but also strengthens their identity as researchers and accelerates their academic and professional journey in AI. .fe-68c23ca7b0cea560b27e5687 { --grid-gutter: calc(var(--sqs-mobile-site-gutter, 6vw) - 11.0px); } } 1,100+ Grants Awarded Every LatinX in AI research workshop is made possible by generous partnerships with individuals and companies committed to equity in AI. Together, we raise funds for the LXAI Travel and Registration Grant Program, which helps remove financial barriers for underrepresented scholars.Our grants are primarily awarded to students from Latin American countries and Hispanic Serving Institutions in the United States—many of whom would not be able to attend without this support. These grants help cover critical costs like travel, lodging, and conference registration, ensuring that brilliant minds from developing regions can present their work, network, and grow their careers.To date, LatinX in AI has awarded over 1,100 conference and travel grants, directly enabling the participation of LatinX researchers and students in the global AI conversation. Our Impact. 10,000+ Members Served LatinX in AI is proud to serve a vibrant, growing network of over 10,000 LatinX researchers, engineers, entrepreneurs, and students across the globe. Through our targeted programs, we are breaking down systemic barriers and expanding opportunities in the field of artificial intelligence.Our community members benefit from:- Research Workshops at top-tier AI conferences- 1:1 and group mentorship across academia and industry- Collaborative publications via our open-access research journal- Access to AI compute resources for under-resourced researchers and studentsTogether, these programs form a powerful ecosystem for professional growth, academic advancement, and cross-border collaboration—amplifying LatinX voices and talent in AI worldwide. 30+ Countries Represented LatinX in AI is a truly global community, representing all Latin-identified countries across North, Central, and South America, the Caribbean, and beyond. Our members span academic institutions, tech companies, and research labs in over 30 countries, including Brazil, Mexico, Colombia, Argentina, Peru, Chile, Venezuela, the Dominican Republic, and many others.We also proudly serve LatinX individuals across the diaspora—including those based in the United States, Canada, Spain, and the UK—who identify with and support our mission to create a more inclusive AI ecosystem.By uniting voices from across borders, languages, and cultures, LatinX in AI fosters cross-regional collaboration, shared knowledge, and a collective effort to shape the future of artificial intelligence in a way that reflects the diversity of our world. .fe-68c2358ee1f8ff7b36c5c3f0 { --grid-gutter: calc(var(--sqs-mobile-site-gutter, 6vw) - 11.0px); communities.description: Six diversity-focused AI communities: WAI (20,000+ members, 130+ countries, 40+ chapters, June 2025 NA Summit 500+); WiMLDS (~100 chapters, 50,000+ members, 501(c)(3), NYC chapter 4,777 members); Black in AI (5,900+ members, 117 countries, NeurIPS workshop, MacArthur grant); LatinX in AI (NeurIPS/ICRA/ACL workshops); Queer in AI (1,000+ Slack, ACL first archival papers 2025); AI4ALL (Dr. Fei-Fei Li co-founded, Stanford/UCSF summer programs).

} } Our Programs. LEARN MORE #block-459a67713ae5b05a35d0 {--sqs-block-content-flex: 0; } Research Workshops at Top AI Conferences LatinX in AI hosts flagship research workshops at premier AI conferences—including NeurIPS, ICML, CVPR, and NAACL—to amplify the voices and contributions of LatinX researchers on the global stage. These workshops provide a welcoming platform for scholars at all stages to present original research, gain visibility, and engage in technical dialogue with the broader AI community.Each workshop features:Peer-reviewed research papers spanning machine learning, computer vision, NLP, ethics, and moreInvited talks from leading LatinX scientists and industry pioneersMentoring sessions, panels, and networking designed to build community and advance careersThrough these workshops, LatinX in AI has built a recognized space for collaboration, visibility, and scholarly excellence—helping hundreds of researchers develop confidence, connect with peers, and access global opportunities in AI. Mentorship That Transforms Careers LatinX in AI’s Mentorship Program connects emerging LatinX talent with experienced researchers, engineers, founders, and industry professionals across the globe. Our goal is to create pathways for growth, confidence, and opportunity in the field of AI.Through structured 1:1 mentorship, group sessions, and peer circles, we support mentees in areas such as:Graduate school applications and academic publishingCareer transitions and technical interviewsResearch feedback and professional developmentWith hundreds of matches made across borders and disciplines, our mentorship network is a cornerstone of our mission—building bridges between generations of LatinX AI leaders and cultivating a more inclusive future for artificial intelligence. Journal of LatinX in AI Research (JLXAIR) The LatinX in AI Research Journal is an open-access publication dedicated to showcasing original work from LatinX researchers across the artificial intelligence landscape. Our journal provides a platform for emerging voices, interdisciplinary ideas, and socially impactful research that often goes underrepresented in mainstream venues.Through regular issues and conference-aligned special editions, we feature:Peer-reviewed papers spanning machine learning, ethics, computer vision, NLP, and moreStudent and early-career research highlightsCollaborative works between academia, industry, and civil societyBy centering LatinX perspectives in AI, our journal not only elevates scholarship but also builds visibility, confidence, and academic credibility for contributors—especially those from developing countries and underfunded institutions. Supercomputer Access Network Through the LXAI Supercomputer Access Network, we provide LatinX researchers, students, and educators with free or low-cost access to cutting-edge AI computing infrastructure—including GPU clusters and supercomputing resources—via partnerships with academic institutions, industry donors, and national labs.This initiative addresses one of the most critical barriers in AI research: lack of access to high-performance computing. Our network enables under-resourced scholars, particularly from Latin America and other developing regions, to:Train large-scale AI modelsRun complex experimentsCollaborate on globally competitive research projectsBy democratizing access to compute, we’re leveling the playing field and fostering a new generation of LatinX innovators in artificial intelligence. LXAI Podcast: Voices of the LatinX AI Community The LatinX in AI Podcast is a storytelling platform that amplifies the voices of LatinX leaders, researchers, entrepreneurs, and changemakers in the field of artificial intelligence. Through in-depth interviews and topical discussions, we explore the intersections of technology, identity, equity, and innovation—bringing forward stories that are too often left out of the AI narrative.Each episode highlights:Breakthrough research and startup journeysInsights into ethical AI, policy, and social impactThe lived experiences of LatinX professionals navigating the tech worldOur podcast builds connection across borders and disciplines while inspiring the next generation of LatinX talent. Whether you’re in academia, industry, or just starting out, the LXAI Podcast offers valuable perspectives from across our global community. LATAM Undergraduate Research Program In collaboration with Google Explore CSR and LatinX in AI, the LATAM Undergraduate Research Program empowers underrepresented undergraduate (and master’s-level) students in computer science and related fields. Through a semester-long mentorship and project-based experience, students develop confidence, critical research skills, and a stronger sense of belonging in the AI community.What participants can expect:Hands-on research projects under the guidance of experienced mentorsOnline workshops, training, and peer activities designed to improve research methods, technical skills, and academic readinessSupport aimed at motivating students to pursue graduate studies in computing and AIThis program not only equips students with practical experience but also strengthens their identity as researchers and accelerates their academic and professional journey in AI. .fe-68c23ca7b0cea560b27e5687 { --grid-gutter: calc(var(--sqs-mobile-site-gutter, 6vw) - 11.0px); } } 1,100+ Grants Awarded Every LatinX in AI research workshop is made possible by generous partnerships with individuals and companies committed to equity in AI. Together, we raise funds for the LXAI Travel and Registration Grant Program, which helps remove financial barriers for underrepresented scholars.Our grants are primarily awarded to students from Latin American countries and Hispanic Serving Institutions in the United States—many of whom would not be able to attend without this support. These grants help cover critical costs like travel, lodging, and conference registration, ensuring that brilliant minds from developing regions can present their work, network, and grow their careers.To date, LatinX in AI has awarded over 1,100 conference and travel grants, directly enabling the participation of LatinX researchers and students in the global AI conversation. Our Impact. 10,000+ Members Served LatinX in AI is proud to serve a vibrant, growing network of over 10,000 LatinX researchers, engineers, entrepreneurs, and students across the globe. Through our targeted programs, we are breaking down systemic barriers and expanding opportunities in the field of artificial intelligence.Our community members benefit from:- Research Workshops at top-tier AI conferences- 1:1 and group mentorship across academia and industry- Collaborative publications via our open-access research journal- Access to AI compute resources for under-resourced researchers and studentsTogether, these programs form a powerful ecosystem for professional growth, academic advancement, and cross-border collaboration—amplifying LatinX voices and talent in AI worldwide. 30+ Countries Represented LatinX in AI is a truly global community, representing all Latin-identified countries across North, Central, and South America, the Caribbean, and beyond. Our members span academic institutions, tech companies, and research labs in over 30 countries, including Brazil, Mexico, Colombia, Argentina, Peru, Chile, Venezuela, the Dominican Republic, and many others.We also proudly serve LatinX individuals across the diaspora—including those based in the United States, Canada, Spain, and the UK—who identify with and support our mission to create a more inclusive AI ecosystem.By uniting voices from across borders, languages, and cultures, LatinX in AI fosters cross-regional collaboration, shared knowledge, and a collective effort to shape the future of artificial intelligence in a way that reflects the diversity of our world. .fe-68c2358ee1f8ff7b36c5c3f0 { --grid-gutter: calc(var(--sqs-mobile-site-gutter, 6vw) - 11.0px); We will cover the costs of travel to/from London, any visa expenses, accommodation for the full duration of the programme, and provide meals, drinks and snacks on programme days. We aim to ensure that finances do not present a barrier to anyone’s participation in ARENA.If you’re interested in applying to future iterations of ARENA but won’t be able to attend ARENA 8.0, please fill in this expression of interest form to ensure you’re kept up-to-date for when we open applications for future iterations.For more information about the programme, see our announcement post on LessWrong. #block-yui_3_17_2_1_1751882471278_3021 { --tweak-text-block-radius: 0px 0px 0px 0px; } } Programme Details: #block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { --tweak-text-block-radius: 0px 0px 0px 0px; --tweak-text-block-radius: unset; --stroke-style: none;--stroke-thickness: 6px; --tweak-text-block-background-color: initial; } #block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 .sqs-html-content { --tweak-text-block-padding: 6% 6% 6% 6%; --tweak-text-block-padding: initial; } .fe-block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { mix-blend-mode: var(--tweak-text-block-blend ); } .fe-block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { --tweak-text-block-blend : normal; } .fe-block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { --blur-value: blur(15px);} #block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { @media screen and (max-width: 767px) { } }#block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 .sqs-html-content { @media screen and (max-width: 767px) { } }@media screen and (max-width: 767px) { } How do we operate? ARENA provides in-person programming bootcamps, equipping people with the skills, community and confidence to contribute to technical AI safety. We run 2-3 bootcamps each year, and each lasts for 4-5 weeks. We’re based at the London Initiative for Safe AI (LISA) in Shoreditch, London.For those who wish to use our materials – whether to learn independently, or run their own AI safety courses – our curriculum is available online.We are grateful to be funded by Coefficient Giving. Who should apply to ARENA? There’s no single profile that we look for at ARENA; in recent iterations, successful applicants have come from diverse academic and professional backgrounds. We intend to keep it this way – this diversity makes our bootcamps a more enriching learning experience for all.When assessing applications to our programme, we like to see:Applicants who genuinely care about AI safety and making the future development of AI go well;Applicants who are able to code well in Python, and have some knowledge of the maths needed for modern AI (linear algebra, calculus, probability);A solid understanding of how you might best contribute to technical AI safety, and how you expect ARENA to help you achieve your goals.Since ARENA is an ML bootcamp, some level of technical skill in maths and coding will be required – more detail on this can be found in our FAQs. However, if our work resonates with you, we encourage you to apply. How might I benefit from ARENA? ARENA can accelerate people’s journey into impactful AI safety work by providing them with the skills, connections, and confidence required to take their next steps. Being situated at LISA also helps our participants build great relationships with people from across the AI safety community.Our alumni have gone onto:Work as AI safety engineers at Apollo Research, Anthropic and METR;Become MATS scholars and LASR participants;Start their own AI safety organisations;Pursue impactful research at world-class academic institutions.During ARENA, we run careers events with a view to helping participants with what comes next. We endeavour to keep in touch with our alumni and look after them beyond their time with us. How do I apply? Please find our applications page here.For our in-person programmes, we operate a three-stage applications process:Initial application form (this should take fewer than 90 minutes);Coding test (six questions within 1-hour time limit);Final interview: a 30-minute discussion with one of our team members exploring your interests, motivations, and technical skills. .fe-67e146e132bcbc72c7a584d6 { --grid-gutter: calc(var(--sqs-mobile-site-gutter, 6vw) - 11.0px); We will cover the costs of travel to/from London, any visa expenses, accommodation for the full duration of the programme, and provide meals, drinks and snacks on programme days. We aim to ensure that finances do not present a barrier to anyone’s participation in ARENA.If you’re interested in applying to future iterations of ARENA but won’t be able to attend ARENA 8.0, please fill in this expression of interest form to ensure you’re kept up-to-date for when we open applications for future iterations.For more information about the programme, see our announcement post on LessWrong. #block-yui_3_17_2_1_1751882471278_3021 { --tweak-text-block-radius: 0px 0px 0px 0px; } #block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 .sqs-html-content { --tweak-text-block-padding: 6% 6% 6% 6%; --tweak-text-block-padding: initial; } .fe-block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { mix-blend-mode: var(--tweak-text-block-blend ); } .fe-block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { --tweak-text-block-blend : normal; } .fe-block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { --blur-value: blur(15px);} #block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { @media screen and (max-width: 767px) { } }#block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 .sqs-html-content { @media screen and (max-width: 767px) { } }@media screen and (max-width: 767px) { } How do we operate? ARENA provides in-person programming bootcamps, equipping people with the skills, community and confidence to contribute to technical AI safety. We run 2-3 bootcamps each year, and each lasts for 4-5 weeks. We’re based at the London Initiative for Safe AI (LISA) in Shoreditch, London.For those who wish to use our materials – whether to learn independently, or run their own AI safety courses – our curriculum is available online.We are grateful to be funded by Coefficient Giving. Who should apply to ARENA? There’s no single profile that we look for at ARENA; in recent iterations, successful applicants have come from diverse academic and professional backgrounds. We intend to keep it this way – this diversity makes our bootcamps a more enriching learning experience for all.When assessing applications to our programme, we like to see:Applicants who genuinely care about AI safety and making the future development of AI go well;Applicants who are able to code well in Python, and have some knowledge of the maths needed for modern AI (linear algebra, calculus, probability);A solid understanding of how you might best contribute to technical AI safety, and how you expect ARENA to help you achieve your goals.Since ARENA is an ML bootcamp, some level of technical skill in maths and coding will be required – more detail on this can be found in our FAQs. However, if our work resonates with you, we encourage you to apply. How might I benefit from ARENA? ARENA can accelerate people’s journey into impactful AI safety work by providing them with the skills, connections, and confidence required to take their next steps. Being situated at LISA also helps our participants build great relationships with people from across the AI safety community.Our alumni have gone onto:Work as AI safety engineers at Apollo Research, Anthropic and METR;Become MATS scholars and LASR participants;Start their own AI safety organisations;Pursue impactful research at world-class academic institutions.During ARENA, we run careers events with a view to helping participants with what comes next. We endeavour to keep in touch with our alumni and look after them beyond their time with us. How do I apply? Please find our applications page here.For our in-person programmes, we operate a three-stage applications process:Initial application form (this should take fewer than 90 minutes);Coding test (six questions within 1-hour time limit);Final interview: a 30-minute discussion with one of our team members exploring your interests, motivations, and technical skills.

communities.name: EA Global
communities.description: Five safety-focused communities: Mox (SF's largest AI safety space, 377 events in past year, partners MIRI/FAR.AI/Redwood); EA Global (application-based conferences in SF Feb 2026 + NYC Oct 2025, 1,000+ attendees); ARENA (4–5 week ML bootcamp London Jan–Feb 2026); AI Safety London (200 members); Stanford AI Alignment (weekly meetings with UC Berkeley/UCLA/Caltech).

width: 100%; height: 100%; display: none; } .js-content-mode-element-system_desktop { display: block; } } ARENA 8.0: May 25 – Jun 26 2026 We are delighted to announce that our programme’s eighth iteration, ARENA 8.0, will be taking place in summer 2026!Applications to ARENA 8.0 are now closed.ARENA 8.0 will be running in-person at the London Initiative for Safe AI (LISA) from May 25th – June 26th. We will cover the costs of travel to/from London, any visa expenses, accommodation for the full duration of the programme, and provide meals, drinks and snacks on programme days. We aim to ensure that finances do not present a barrier to anyone’s participation in ARENA.If you’re interested in applying to future iterations of ARENA but won’t be able to attend ARENA 8.0, please fill in this expression of interest form to ensure you’re kept up-to-date for when we open applications for future iterations.For more information about the programme, see our announcement post on LessWrong. .fe-67e146e632bcbc72c7a58578 { --grid-gutter: calc(var(--sqs-mobile-site-gutter, 6vw) - 11.0px);
